actions.detail: role 'Claims Analyst' bound to permission group
role 'Carrier Compliance Analyst' bound to permission group
role 'Safety Review Specialist' bound to permission group
role 'Claims & Safety Manager' bound to permission group
role 'Fleet Compliance Auditor' bound to permission group

deferred.reason: Claims-and-safety-domain policies (no claim payment, denial, or recovery filing communicated before the human determination; denial letters must cite the documented investigation findings and the applicable bill-of-lading defense; no preventability determination recorded without the event evidence attached; suspected double-broker loads freeze carrier payment pending verification) require guardrail-service / OPA policy materialization, which Core does not expose as a pack surface yet. The behavioral core is partially covered today by Core invariants: every pack-configured agent is proposal-mode with four-eyes approval (agent-runtime), and the agent instructions + disposition taxonomy route final determinations exclusively to human analysts with the Claims & Safety Manager as approver.

Bespoke LangGraph recipes (Cargo Claim Intake Copilot, Carrier Recovery Builder, Double-Broker Fraud Screener, Filing-Deadline Sentinel, Preventability Review Assistant) require agent-runtime graph-module registration (BRD 23 BR-12). This pack specializes the existing case-triage + analytics agents via TenantAgentConfig prompt_params + tenant-scope RAG grounding instead.

Production read connectors (TMS platforms: McLeod/TMW/MercuryGate-class systems; telematics and video-safety platforms: ELD, forward-camera, harsh-event feeds; FMCSA registration/safety data services; load-board and carrier-identity monitoring; reefer telematics/temperature-download providers; claims mailboxes/EDI) require customer credentials and pack-service connector materialization. The pack ships seed datasets in the exact landing shape; production connectors are configured through the product Data > Connections UI.

Write adapters (issue claim payment or settlement in the TMS/AP system, send acknowledgment/denial letters, file recovery claims against upstream carriers, suspend a carrier in the carrier-management system, assign driver coaching in the telematics platform) execute only behind pack-service's materialization contract and customer credentials; every such write stays proposal-mode with human approval — no autonomous claim payment, carrier suspension, or coaching assignment, ever.

Golden eval sets (OS&D claim triage, Carmack liability-band assignment, deadline-risk ranking, double-broker red-flag detection, preventability recommendation quality) require eval-service write APIs whose action names are not yet in the rbac canonical catalog (Core follow-up noted in rbac catalog.go).

Ontology registration (Shipper, Consignee, Carrier, Driver, Shipment, Lane, BillOfLading, Claim, SafetyEvent, RecoveryClaim, SealRecord, TemperatureLog, PreventabilityDetermination, CoachingRecord) awaits pack-service; entity semantics are carried today by the trucking_claims_core semantic model and dataset schemas.

Typed case schemas (cargo_osd_claim, reefer_excursion_review, safety_event_review, carrier_fraud_vetting, recovery_filing) await case-service schema materialization; today's cases carry the same fields via display_projection + the disposition taxonomy.

Model archetypes (claim_outcome_confidence, carrier_fraud_risk_score, preventability_likelihood) await experiment-service archetype APIs; the scoring capability ships as pipeline seeds instead (isolation_forest shipment anomaly + xgboost claim-outcome scorer) with model-governance expectations noted in grounding memories.

Display-label overlays ("Claim", "Safety event", "Filing deadline at risk", "Carrier on watch", entity templates) require the BFF label-registry endpoints (BFF-FR-082/088) which are pack-service-tier features.
